sentences.text: Hi, Chris!
No worries.
Chris.
I'm good, how are you?
Good.
… How's your week going?
Not too bad, how about you?
Same.

It's been, … I've been trying to manage, so my son, my 7-year-old, he starts school next week, and I didn't have enough for camps this week, so I'm trying to navigate between working, having him home, stuff like that.
So, it's been… it's been a little chaotic this week.
Yeah.
Hi, Tabitha.
Hello?
How are you?
Good, how are you?
Good.
Okay, well, you both should have received an invite about 20 minutes ago.
It was sent over.
I don't know if it comes… I… who does it come from?
Does it come from Rentable or ApartmentIQ?
ApartmentIQ.
Okay, perfect.
So there's a link in there, and that should give you access to log in, create a password, so just let me know once you've gotten that far.
Tabitha, did you receive it?
I did.
I was blocking some content, so I'm trying to….
I'm… I'm good to go on my end.
Oh, fabulous.

So I figured we would just use this one to make sure you're able to get in, because sometimes we have users that either they can't find the invite, or they can't figure out how to get in there, but then I'm just going to show you some basic functions, and then there was one call-out I wanted to make with Whispering Point.
Chris, I'll show you this while Tabitha's doing that.
So Whispering Point, it doesn't look like you guys are displaying pricing and availability on the website.
So right here, you're gonna see Limited Data Tag.
You can click that, and it will show you that we're collecting 75 units, but there isn't any pricing or dates available or anything like that on the website.

So I just wanted to let you guys know that, so it's gonna look… excuse me… it's gonna look a little bit different than the other three properties, because there's not gonna be… A ton of… there's not gonna be pricing information, because there's nothing for us to pull.
How… how often does that sync run?
Every day.
So it runs overnight, every night from 10 until 7am.
Okay, so that should be on there tomorrow, then, but this is… yeah, it's on the docket to be set up.
I figured it's a lease-up property, so I figured, like, that's… I took that with a grain of salt.
So I just… when you look at this property today, I just wanted to let you know that right now, there was nothing for us to pull over in regards to the pricing.
We're showing 75 units, but again, no pricing on there for those properties, so… Just a little heads up there for you.
Okay, so you guys are both in.
Something I'll show you over here on the left-hand side, I did turn on Explore for you, so this is the market, sub-market data.
You can… you can search by a neighborhood, a zip code, a property address, and it will filter by radius.
So you do have that in there.

And then also on the left-hand side, under Manage Users, right now I just have the two of you enabled, but if you wanted to add a new user, you could invite new user, enter in their information, and then the role, this will just give you, when you select it, it gives you a little flip about what they'll have access to.
So, admin has access to everything, so they can edit comp sets, they can add new users, they can make all of the changes, and same thing.
When you… when you select the different permissions, it will give you that.
So, if you're planning on adding additional users, that's how you would go ahead and do that.
So, I will just give a quick overview, so we'll use Swift Water Apartments.
So, just as a reminder, up here is going to be your main navigation, so this is going to give you access to all the different reports.
So, think of this, the market survey is going to be the high-level floor plans, granular unit level information, and then the historical information as well.

You do have different filtering options, so you can filter by different properties, like if you just want to look at a couple of the comps at a time, or you want to filter by different bedrooms, bathrooms, combinations, things like that.
And then, a big call-out here is customized metrics.
We automatically toggle off a bunch of metrics, not to make it too busy.
So, this is user-specific.
So, Chris, whatever you have toggled on will not affect Tabitha's view and vice versa, so you can go through and turn on whatever additional metrics you want.

… what I suggest, because you guys currently have 4 properties in this, you can apply customized view to all market surveys, and whatever you select here, it will apply those additional metrics to all of… All four properties.
Up here in the top right, you did provide us comps, but if you want to add more comps, you would just say edit comps.
You've got the map view on the right, your property is here in purple, properties in the comp sets are already… are the white pins, and then black pins are additional properties in the area.
You also have it over here in a list view.
Or, you can search by ad comp, and you can search by the property name or the address.
What… what score do you guys recommend, typically?
Like, the cutoffs.

Yeah, the comp score, that is… I have to say this, take that with a grain of salt, that is a computer-generated score, so that is… that is literally based off of the proximity, the year built or renovated, how many units are in the property, we do some pricing and amenity matching as well.
So that is all just computer-derived.

You guys know your comps best, so… but if you were going into a market and maybe… either it was a new market, or maybe you were brand new to the company, this will just give you some guidance as to properties that you could consider adding.
So that's what this score is.
It's just a computer-generated score based off of those aspects.

So you can add them just… if you want to remove any of these, you would just X them off, or you can simply click Add, or over on the map view, you can hover over it, and that's not a great comp, obviously, but you could just say add comp, and it will instantly be added.
You just refresh your screen, and it will appear there in your comp set.
Is this… is this individualized as well?

The comps are not, so that's one permission where, the read-only and the manager access, you don't allow comp editing, so that is across the board, so that will affect both of you, or whatever, however many users you guys have in the application.
But that at least gets us all looking at the same set.
Again, you may focus on different properties, but….
Correct.
Yes.
Okay.
… to download the Excel and PDF reports.
That's super easy, you can either download, or you can schedule them to your inbox.
on a cadence, whatever you'd like.
Just keep in mind, for the Excel report, whatever filters you have here.
So if you only have we'll say 2 properties toggled on, or 3 properties toggled on, and you don't have certain metrics, whatever you're seeing here is what's going to carry over to the Excel.
So, whatever you want on that Excel report, make sure that you're viewing it in the app.
Just… just as adds up.
All of this information you can drill into.
So, if you want to see the 92 units that we're showing as available, you click into that.
And here are the units.
The data available, the pricing, and then any concessions that we're capturing.
The pricing that's showing, is that just, like, a minimum rent you're seeing, or what is the….
This is what you guys are advertising online.
For this specific unit.
So, our rents obviously change based on, like, several different factors, so is it… Just whatever is showing as, like, a starting at rate for that individual unit.
So, if you guys have… let's… let me go to your website.
So, it should be unit-specific, so we are pulling what you guys have as….
As, like, a 12-month lease, is that what you guys are typically….
That's correct.
Yes.
Okay.
Yes.
But yeah, so whatever… if you're seeing a discrepancy, let me know, because our team's gonna look into it, because whatever we're pulling is coming from the public data, specifically your website.
So if you're seeing a discrepancy, definitely reach out and let me know.
So if something isn't matching up, let me know that, okay?
Okay.
Okay, so I showed you the reports, editing comps, adding users, accessing additional metrics…
Anything else you wanted to quick… oh, okay, so here's another one.
So this property here… It was probably toggled off.
So this is showing, if you hover over limited data.
I don't know why it's not doing it right now, that's strange.
It will… it gives you a pop-up, and it says that this property… there we go.
This property appears to be withholding units from its advertisements, reducing the metric's accuracy below our standard.
So it's just saying, we believe that they are hiding units, we're still gonna show you, if you click on that, we're still gonna show you what we're capturing, but just take that with a grain of salt.
It's a lease-up property, so they might not be releasing their full availability.
So, that's just what that means.
Okay.
… Yeah, that was really it.
I just wanted to make sure that you guys were able to log in, knew how to access additional metrics and different reports and stuff like that.
Do you have any questions for me while we're here?
Off the top of my head, no, I think it's going to be exploring around a little bit.
I can see where it shows, like, the historicals and that kind of stuff, because I know it'll be interesting to see what information you guys show compared to some of the stuff we have, so….
Great.
If any questions come up while you're exploring the tool, definitely reach out.
How long do you want access for?
And are there any other stakeholders that you would want a demo for to see the tool?
Because we're more than happy to set that up as well.
I don't know at this point, Chris, if you disagree, let me know.
I don't know at this point we're ready to set up another demo.
I think, I'll probably give our… new market rate asset manager access.
I'll give Marianne access so that she can look at it as well.
But I don't know that we're at a point of setting up a demo for somebody to try and….
I agree.
fold through this until we have some time to review the product a little bit more.
What is your typical demo time?

Typically… 2 weeks, some customers go up to 30 days, so whatever your preference is, I mean, we're working with you, so it's… you guys are figuring out if this is a tool that's gonna work for your portfolio, so whatever time frame is suitable, I just like to have that just for fall perspective and things like that, checking in and timelines like that, so… Whatever works best for you guys.
I'd like to see it for probably 30 days.
Chris, do you agree?
Yeah.

Especially with coming into a holiday, we're coming into some budget season time, that kind of thing, just to give us time to have the ability to really dig in and make sure that this is… see if this is a tool we, can pitch that we need.
Of course.
What I will do, I'm gonna make a note to follow up with you in 2 weeks.
I'll just reach out via email just to see how things are going.
By all means, do not wait 2 weeks.
If you have questions or concerns or anything like that, reach out to me ahead of time.
But if I don't hear from you, I'll reach out in 2 weeks.

And then at that point, we'll just… I'll check in, see how things are going, and… We'll see if at that point you guys are interested in scheduling another demo, or if you want to wait an additional 2 weeks, we can schedule a call to check in at that 30-day mark, just to see how things are going, and we can go from there, so….
Sounds good?
Perfect.
I appreciate your time.
Let me know if things come up, and I will talk to you both in a couple of weeks.
Thanks so much!
Alright, perfect, thank you.
